entry.title: Re: SAP RDP: ES Plastic Tax - Missing Tax ID for delivier...
entry.content:
The Spanish Plastic Tax report uses material documents (such as goods receipts and goods issues) as its source of information. These documents carry supplier tax IDs, which is why the VAT number is shown correctly for goods receipts.

For deliveries, however, the system does not always include tax ID fields for the supplying warehouse or plant. This is standard system behavior, not a defect. The report is designed to reflect acquisitions from suppliers, not internal plant transfers. As a result, the VAT number of the German warehouse is not automatically displayed in the EU Inventory Book.

Options to address this:

  • Ensure that VAT registration numbers are correctly maintained in the master data of the supplying plant/company.
  • If business requires the VAT number of the German warehouse to appear, a small enhancement may be needed to enrich the report with plant VAT information during delivery postings.

entry.title: Re: Introducing Custom Events in SAP Cloud ALM Health Monitoring
entry.content:
Dear Qendrim,

Thank you for introducing Custom Events in SAP Cloud ALM Health Monitoring — this is a very valuable enhancement.

I would like to ask whether there are plans in the near future to support additional filtering capabilities for custom events or metrics.

For example, for the Response Time metric, it would be extremely useful to be able to filter by technical attributes such as:

  • TaskType == Dialog

This would allow more granular monitoring and analysis, especially in environments where different task types have very different performance characteristics.

Is this kind of attribute-based filtering on the roadmap, or being considered for upcoming releases?

Thank you in advance for any insights you can share.

entry.title: Your Cloud Future Starts Here: Core Capabilities of SAP BTP Explained
entry.content:
A New Expert Series for Architects, Developers, and IT Leaders

The cloud journey isn’t a single leap. It’s more like assembling a high-performance engine while it’s already running.

With SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) at the center, organizations are not just moving to the cloud—they’re building intelligent, integrated, and future-ready landscapes. But to truly unlock its value, understanding the core capabilities is essential.

SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) continues to evolve at a rapid pace—bringing new capabilities for integration, automation, security, governance, and application development. With so much innovation happening across the platform, staying up to date can feel like a full‑time job.

To help you navigate this landscape, we’re launching a new expert-led series:

 Your Cloud Future Starts Here: Core Capabilities of SAP BTP Explained

Across multiple deep-dive sessions, SAP product managers and engineering experts will walk you through the latest capabilities, best practices, and strategic direction of SAP BTP. Whether you’re modernizing landscapes, building cloud-native extensions, or strengthening your security posture, this series gives you the insights you need to succeed.

Each session is live, free, and open to the entire SAP Community—with demos, roadmaps, and the opportunity to learn directly from the teams building SAP BTP.

Below is your complete overview of all upcoming sessions.

 

Apr 21 — Govern your cloud deliveries with SAP Cloud ALM and SAP Cloud Transport Management

 

Learn about the latest innovations in SAP Cloud ALM and SAP Cloud Transport Management Service, including modifiable transport requests in SAP BTP. See how real-time adjustments streamline change management across cloud landscapes.

Why You Should Join This Series

This series is designed for anyone shaping their organization’s cloud strategy on SAP BTP:

  • Developers looking to build secure, scalable cloud apps

the easiest way would be to use Fiori app Transfer Stock - in plant (F1061). Simply select the Project Stock (Q) in in Source Storage location, select the target Storage Location and in the subsequent pop up enter the quantity rsp adjust the WBS Element in case more than one Special Stock Type Project Stock with different WBS element exists (See attachment).

entry.title: Re: Migration Objects listed in Help Portal but not avail...
entry.content:
Hello,

Thank you for bringing up this migration object visibility issue. I can confirm that all 9 Direct Transfer migration objects you’ve listed should indeed be available in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition systems:

  1. Business partner (Direct Transfer)
  2. MM - Purchasing info record (Direct Transfer)
  3. Object classification (general template) (Direct Transfer)
  4. PP - Material BOM (Direct Transfer)
  5. PP - Production order (Direct Transfer)
  6. PP - Production version (Direct Transfer)
  7. PP - Routing (Direct Transfer)
  8. PP - Work center (Direct Transfer)
  9. Pricing condition (sales) (Direct Transfer)

Since these objects are documented as supported and should be accessible via Fiori App F8638 (Migration Object Search), but are not appearing in your system, this appears to be a system-specific issue rather than expected behavior.

Recommendation:

Please raise a support ticket with SAP (component: CA-DT-MIG-S4C) to investigate why these Direct Transfer migration objects are not visible in your system. When creating the ticket, include:

• Your system name, client number, and product version
• Screenshots of the F8638 search results showing the missing objects
• Reference to the Help Portal documentation URL you provided
• Complete list of the missing objects

The Direct Transfer approach is not deprecated for Public Edition, and you should have access to these migration objects as documented.

Best regards

entry.title: Re: Material Long Description for search help while creat...
entry.content: By default, ME21N search help shows short descriptions only. To display long descriptions, you need to enhance or create a custom search help that includes the long text field. This is a standard enhancement in Private Cloud and does not require major customization.

entry.title: Cumulative Calculation Across Blended Models in SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC)
entry.content:
Hi all,
I am working with three models in SAP Analytics Cloud: Sales, Comp Stock, and Purchase. Each model contains a YearMonth dimension, and I have linked these models in a story using the Link Dimensions (Blending) option. Additionally, Material ID is also used as a linking dimension.

Requirement:
I need to calculate cumulative values (e.g., Quantity, Sales, Cost, Comp Stock) based on the selected YearMonth:

  • If Dec 2025 is selected → cumulative should be from Jan 2025 to Dec 2025
  • If Jan 2026 is selected → cumulative should be from Feb 2025 to Jan 2026 (rolling 12 months)
  • If no YearMonth is selected → it should default to cumulative up to the current month

Issue:
I have tried the following approaches, but none are working correctly due to model blending:

  • Running Total
  • Restricted Measures
  • Aggregation Calculations

These either fail to respect the blended model context or do not compute correctly across models.

Question:
What is the recommended approach to calculate cumulative (especially rolling 12-month) values across blended models in SAC?
Is there a way to handle this via calculated measures, advanced formulas, or model-level changes?

Any guidance or best practices would be greatly appreciated.

entry.title: Re: Excess Quantity Not Allowed For Production Order
entry.content: In Public Cloud, you cannot confirm more than the planned quantity. To resolve the error, increase the production order quantity (not just the component requirement) so that confirmations remain consistent. This is the only standard way to handle excess quantity confirmations in Public Cloud.

entry.title: Re: SAP Datasphere: Extract, Transform & Load (ETL) - Best Practices
entry.content:
Hi Jeet,

Thank you for your contribution. I had been looking for an article like this for quite a while, especially one that provides such a clear and comprehensive overview of the different features available for ETL Flows.

Based on my experience with SAP Datasphere so far, I would like to share a few considerations on some of the points raised in your article.

I have always used Data Flow instead of Replication Flow or Transformation Flow. The reason is that Data Flow allows me to load data processed by a view created in Datasphere into a Local Table. I tried to build the same pipeline using Replication Flow, but I found that it is not possible to select Datasphere local repository objects as source and target in the same flow.

Of course, I may be overlooking something, but if that is indeed the case, then Data Flow currently seems to be the only feature that makes it possible to set up a periodic historization process for query result sets by appending them to a Local Table.

Another observation concerns the comparison between Replication Flow and Remote Table Replication.

I am struggling to understand how these two objects can really be compared. When we talk about Remote Tables, this feature is already natively available in the Data Builder and can then be managed and monitored through the Data Integration Monitor. For this reason, I do not really see the benefit of creating and configuring an additional artifact, such as a Replication Flow, to perform what appears to be the same task.

This is just my point of view, and I would be very interested to hear your perspective on it.

Thank you again.

entry.title: Avoid Using a Slash in Product Codes in SAP Commerce Cloud
entry.content:
In SAP Commerce Cloud, using a forward slash (/) in Product or GenericVariantProduct codes can lead to unexpected issues, such as broken product pages and failed search results. This behavior is not a defect; rather, it is a consequence of standard web protocols and the underlying architecture of SAP Commerce Cloud. 

The following sections explain why we recommend avoiding the use of slashes in product codes.


1. How URLs Are Processed (Apache Is the First Barrier)


When a user clicks a product link, the request flow is:

Browser → Apache Web Server → Tomcat/Spring → Commerce components (Solr, search services, etc.)

Apache processes requests before they reach SAP Commerce. By default, the Apache directive AllowEncodedSlashes is set to Off, which means:

Forward slashes (/), backslashes (\), encoded slashes (%2F), or encoded backslashes (%5C) are rejected at the web server level
Requests containing these characters return a 404 (Not Found)
The request never reaches Tomcat, Spring, or any Commerce logic
This behavior is intentional and serves as an important security measure.

While Apache can technically bypass this restriction by setting:

AllowEncodedSlashes NoDecode

 
this only addresses the web server layer and introduces security considerations. More importantly:

  • It does not resolve downstream issues in SAP Commerce
  • It does not change how URLs are interpreted structurally
  • It does not prevent inconsistencies in Solr, routing, or application logic

Framework-level logic (Spring MVC, filters, controllers) executes only after Apache forwards the request, so relying on Apache configuration is neither complete nor recommended.

2. PDP and OCC Endpoints Depend on Path-Based URLs

SAP Commerce PDP (Product Detail Page) and OCC endpoints are GET-based and rely on a path-based URL structure:

/{productCode}
/products/{code}


 If an encoded product code (e.g., ABC%2F123) reaches Commerce, customization is required to handle it properly. When implementing such customizations:

  • Encoding and decoding must be consistently managed at every layer
  • Inconsistent handling can cause subtle errors, data inconsistencies, or unpredictable platform behavior


Alternative approaches, such as using a query parameter (?productCode=ABC/123) or a POST body, may bypass this limitation. However, they are not recommended because they:

  • Require significant architectural changes
  • Disrupt SEO and caching behavior
  • Impact standard out-of-the-box (OOTB) link generation and OCC contracts

Even workarounds like converting ABC/123 → ABC-123 introduce:

  • Custom URL mapping
  • Bidirectional mapping logic (URL ↔ original code)
  • Risk of identifier collisions
  • Ongoing upgrade and maintenance overhead

These approaches deviate from standard SAP Commerce design and are not OOTB supported.

3. Solr Search Is Also Affected


Using slashes in product codes can create search and retrieval issues:

  • Search query mismatch: Queries must be properly encoded/decoded to match indexed values
  • Indexing vs. retrieval inconsistencies: Product codes may not resolve correctly across search, PDP, or APIs
  • Faceting and filtering issues: Slashes can break filter queries or require additional escaping logic

These issues occur within the Commerce application layer, so Apache changes alone cannot resolve them.

4. Data Consistency and Debugging Complexity


Since the issue spans multiple layers:

  • Apache (URL rejection)
  • Commerce routing (path-based PDP)
  • Solr (index/query parsing)

It can lead to hard-to-debug inconsistencies, where the same product behaves differently across:

  • Search results
  • PDP rendering
  • OCC APIs


5. Recommended Approach


To ensure long-term stability and supportability:

Use URL-safe product codes (avoid /, \, %2F, %5C)
If business identifiers require special characters, introduce a URL-safe surrogate key for storefront exposure
Avoid relying on Apache configuration changes as a workaround
Following these practices ensures consistent, reliable, and supportable behavior across SAP Commerce Cloud.

entry.title: Re: Asset Report not executed in ECC
entry.content:
Hi @Reshan_Ranasinghe ,
My friend you are seeing this error because SAP Asset Accounting requires a technical fiscal year change (AJRW) to be executed for the company code—even if the fiscal year/periods appear open in OAAQ. Opening periods in OAAQ alone is not enough; the system must carry forward asset values into the new fiscal year before asset reports can run.

Please follow the steps mentioned below
1. Go to Transaction  code AJRW
2. Enter Company Code XXX
3. Enter the New Fiscal Year (e.g., 2026)

Run in background (SAP recommends this for performance). Check the log for errors.

Please accept my solution if you resolve the issue by using my solution.

Regards

Re: Need All Characters in Uppercase in DMEE Payment File...
entry.content:
Hi @vijayhiremath ,
My friend In a DMEE tree, the correct way to force all alphanumeric characters to uppercase is to use the standard conversion rule “All characters in upper case (capital letters)”, which is available under Character String conversion rules.

SAP DMEE supports several built‑in conversion rules for character strings. One of them is explicitly designed for your requirement. Conversion Rule to Use is “All characters in upper case (capital letters)”
This is the correct conversion function for uppercase transformation in DMEE.
It is part of the standard conversion options for Character String fields.

Hope above solution will help you. Please accept is it helped you.

entry.title: Release of System-Based Certification for SAP Service Cloud Version 2
entry.content:
Hello everyone, 

On behalf of the product learning team, we are pleased to announce the updated and first of its kind system based certification for SAP Service Cloud Version 2. 

SAP Certified - Implementation Consultant - SAP Service Cloud Version 2

This milestone marks a major shift in how SAP validates real-world skills by moving away from traditional theory-based assessments and toward practical, scenario-driven, hands-on evaluations.

 

🎯Who Is This Certification For?

This certification is designed for:
  • Implementation consultants
  • System administrators

🌟A New Era of Practical, System-Based Assessment

Unlike standard multiple-choice exams, this new certification provides candidates with:
  • A live SAP Service Cloud Version 2 system, and
  • A PDF containing a set of tasks to complete
Candidates perform actual configuration and scenario-based exercises directly in the system. Once all required tasks are completed, they can submit their results for automated evaluation. Within minutes, candidates will know whether they have passed or failed — offering a faster, more authentic, and more meaningful way to validate capability.
This practical approach ensures that certified consultants are not only knowledgeable but also skilled in applying SAP Service Cloud Version 2 functionality in realistic customer scenarios.
 

🎯Why System Based Certifications?

The reimagined SAP Certification exams offer a range of benefits. More specifically:

  • Job-ready proof: Demonstrates you can navigate SAP Service Cloud Version 2 and execute business-critical tasks—not just recall concepts.
  • Strong SAP signal: An SAP-recognised credential and digital badge that hiring managers and project leads across the ecosystem trust.
  • Practice over memorisation: Preparation aligns to SAP Learning Journeys and hands-on exercises for better retention and impact.
  • Always current: Content and recertification align with SAP’s cloud release cycle, reflecting the latest features and UI. 
  • Higher integrity than Q&A-only: Performance on practical tasks reduces guesswork and emphasises authentic ability.

📚How to Prepare

To set yourself up for success, we recommend the following preparation path:

1. Complete the SAP Service Cloud Version 2 Learning Courses

The new learning journey is available on learning.sap.com provide the essential and implementation knowledge you need before attempting the hands‑on tasks. 

2. Practice Using the Hands-On System Exercises

Strengthen your confidence by trying out interactive exercises on a practical system . This helps you become familiar with the interfaces, workflows, and configuration steps you’ll be expected to perform during the certification. 

 

📢Spread the Word!

Help us share this exciting update with partners, colleagues, and other stakeholders.

📝We Value Your Feedback

After completing the certification, please take a moment to share your feedback. Your insights help us continuously improve the learning experience.

entry.title: Re: Bypassing Commitment Item Check for Certain Transactions
entry.content:
Hi @Lael_K ,
My friend it look like you have already done the right checks — the commitment item is correctly derived from the GL account, and FMDERIVE shows the correct result. When FBCJ still throws “No commitment item entered in item 00001 1001”, it usually means the issue is not with derivation itself, but with how the cash journal posting interacts with Funds Management (FM) settings.

Below are the most common root causes and how to fix them. I’ll walk you through them clearly so you can pinpoint the exact one in your system.

1. If Funds Management is active for the company code, SAP will check commitment items for all postings — including cash journal — unless you explicitly exempt them.
Goto SPRO → Public Sector Management → Funds Management → Basic Settings → Activate/Deactivate FM for Applications
Check whether Cash Journal (FBCJ) is active for FM. If you do not want FM checks for FBCJ then
Deactivate FM for Cash Journal for the relevant company code. This immediately stops the commitment item check for FBCJ postings.

2. If the commitment item is derived, SAP may reject it if the financial transaction (FT) on the GL account is incompatible and the commitment item category does not match the posting type. Check In FMGLFLEX or FMDERIVE, verify, Financial transaction (e.g., 30, 40, 50). Commitment item category (e.g., 1 = revenue, 2 = expense, 3 = statistical). Similar issues when Cash journal postings often use financial transaction 30 (Expenditure). If your commitment item is Revenue or Statistical, SAP rejects it.

If you cannot fix with above suggestions you can message me to give you some more suggestions.

entry.title: Re: How to post negative sales in S4
entry.content: In S/4HANA, negative sales from POS should be routed to a special storage location. When stock arrives, the system transfers from the main location to this special location to clear the negative balance. This is the recommended way to handle POS negative sales without enabling negative stock in the main inventory.

entry.title: How I Connected Claude AI to My SAP ABAP System Using MCP — A Complete Windows Guide
entry.content:
Introduction

The SAP developer community has been buzzing about connecting Claude AI directly to live SAP ABAP systems using MCP (Model Context Protocol) — reading code, modifying objects, creating transports, and activating changes, all from a natural language chat interface.

I tried it myself on Windows, and it works. This blog walks you through exactly how to set it up.

What is MCP?

MCP (Model Context Protocol) is an open standard that lets AI models like Claude connect to external tools and data sources. Think of it as a plugin system for AI — instead of just answering questions from training data, Claude can actually call tools that interact with real systems.

For SAP developers, this means Claude can:

  • Read your actual ABAP code directly from your system
  • Write and modify objects
  • Run syntax checks
  • Create transport requests
  • Activate objects

All without you copy-pasting anything.

The Architecture

 
 
Claude Desktop / Claude Code CLI         ↕ MCP Protocol  ┌──────────────────────────┐  │   mcp-abap-adt           │  ← Read-only (13 tools)  │   mcp-abap-abap-adt-api  │  ← Full CRUD (30 tools)  └──────────────────────────┘         ↕ ADT REST API    Your SAP ABAP System

Both MCP servers were built by Mario Andreschak and communicate with SAP via the same ADT (ABAP Development Tools) API that Eclipse uses under the hood.

Prerequisites

Before starting, you need:

  • Windows 10 or 11 (64-bit)
  • An SAP ABAP system with /sap/bc/adt active in SICF
  • A user with SAP_ADT_DEVELOPER role (or equivalent)
  • A Claude Pro/Max/Team subscription at claude.ai
  • Internet connection

Step 1 — Install Node.js

Node.js is the runtime required by Claude Code CLI and the MCP servers.

  1. Go to https://nodejs.org and download the LTS version
  2. Install with all defaults
  3. Verify in Command Prompt:
 
 
node -v    → v24.x.xnpm -v     → 11.x.x

Step 2 — Install Git for Windows

Git provides the bash shell Claude Code requires on Windows.

  1. Go to https://git-scm.com/downloads/win and download the installer
  2. During setup, on the PATH screen select: "Git from the command line and also from 3rd-party software"
  3. Complete with all other defaults

Step 3 — Install Claude Code CLI

 
 
cmd
npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-codeclaude --version

Then log in:

 
 
cmd
claude

Select option 1 (Claude subscription), log in via browser, return to terminal.

Step 4 — Install mcp-abap-adt (Read-Only MCP Server)

This server gives Claude read access to your SAP system with 13 tools including GetProgram, GetClass, GetFunction, GetTable, SearchObject, and more.

 
 
cmd
mkdir C:\Toolscd C:\Toolsgit clone https://github.com/mario-andreschak/mcp-abap-adtcd mcp-abap-adtnpm installnpm run build

Create a .env file in the folder:

 
 
SAP_URL=https://your-sap-system.com:44300SAP_USERNAME=YOUR_USERNAMESAP_PASSWORD=YOUR_PASSWORDSAP_CLIENT=300TLS_REJECT_UNAUTHORIZED=0

Register with Claude Code:

 
 
cmd
claude mcp add mcp-abap-adt node C:/Tools/mcp-abap-adt/dist/index.jsclaude mcp list

You should see: mcp-abap-adt: ✓ Connected

Step 5 — Install mcp-abap-abap-adt-api (Full CRUD Server)

This is the powerful one — 30 tools including read, write, syntax check, activate, lock/unlock, and transport management.

 
 
cmd
cd C:\Toolsgit clone https://github.com/mario-andreschak/mcp-abap-abap-adt-api.gitcd mcp-abap-abap-adt-apinpm installnpm run build

Create .env (note: slightly different variable names):

 
 
SAP_URL=https://your-sap-system.com:44300SAP_USER=YOUR_USERNAMESAP_PASSWORD=YOUR_PASSWORDSAP_CLIENT=300SAP_LANGUAGE=ENNODE_TLS_REJECT_UNAUTHORIZED=0

Register:

 
 
cmd
claude mcp add mcp-abap-adt-api node C:/Tools/mcp-abap-abap-adt-api/dist/index.js

Step 6 — Setup Claude Desktop (GUI Interface)

Claude Desktop is where it really comes alive — a beautiful chat UI like the LinkedIn video.

  1. Download from https://claude.ai/download
  2. Install and log in
  3. Go to ☰ → Settings → Developer → Edit Config
  4. Replace the content with:
 
 
json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "mcp-abap-adt": {
      "command": "C:\\Program Files\\nodejs\\node.exe",
      "args": ["C:\\Tools\\mcp-abap-adt\\dist\\index.js"],
      "env": {
        "SAP_URL": "https://your-sap-system.com:44300",
        "SAP_USERNAME": "YOUR_USERNAME",
        "SAP_PASSWORD": "YOUR_PASSWORD",
        "SAP_CLIENT": "300",
        "TLS_REJECT_UNAUTHORIZED": "0"
      }
    },
    "mcp-abap-adt-api": {
      "command": "C:\\Program Files\\nodejs\\node.exe",
      "args": ["C:\\Tools\\mcp-abap-abap-adt-api\\dist\\index.js"],
      "env": {
        "SAP_URL": "https://your-sap-system.com:44300",
        "SAP_USER": "YOUR_USERNAME",
        "SAP_PASSWORD": "YOUR_PASSWORD",
        "SAP_CLIENT": "300",
        "SAP_LANGUAGE": "EN",
        "NODE_TLS_REJECT_UNAUTHORIZED": "0"
      }
    }
  }
}

⚠️ Windows-specific tip: You MUST use the full path C:\\Program Files\\nodejs\\node.exe — using just "node" does not work in Claude Desktop on Windows. Also note double backslashes \\ in all paths.

  1. Kill Claude in Task Manager and reopen
  2. Go back to Settings → Developer — you should see both servers listed as running ✅

Step 7 — Add Free Official SAP MCP Servers

Bonus: Claude Desktop has a built-in directory of official SAP MCP servers. Click "Connect your tools to Claude" at the bottom of the chat, search for "SAP", and add:

  • SAPUI5 MCP Server — for UI5 development
  • SAP CAP MCP Server — for CAP projects
  • SAP Fiori MCP Server — for Fiori Elements apps

Also add these free hosted servers to Claude Code CLI (no installation needed):

 
 
cmd
claude mcp add --transport http abap-docs https://mcp-abap.marianzeis.de/mcpclaude mcp add --transport http sap-docs https://mcp-sap-docs.marianzeis.de/mcp

These give Claude access to the full ABAP keyword documentation across 8 SAP releases, ABAP cheat sheets, CAP docs, UI5 docs, and SAP Community posts.

Testing It

Open Claude Desktop, create a New Chat, and type:

 
 
Get the source code of program SAPMV45A

Watch Claude automatically call your SAP system, retrieve the source code, and explain it — all in seconds.

For write operations (test on $TMP objects only!):

 
 
Find a simple Z program in $TMP, add a comment at the top saying "Modified by Claude", save and activate it

For complex workflows like the LinkedIn video:

 
 
Connect to my SAP system. Copy class ZCL_SOURCE from package ZPKG_A into ZPKG_B, rename it to ZCL_TARGET, change the carrier_id in the SELECT for /DMO/CONNECTION, create a transport and activate.

Security Notes

  • Never commit .env files to Git
  • Change your SAP password if shared in screenshots
  • Use dedicated service accounts with minimum required authorizations
  • Only test write operations in $TMP before moving to real packages
  • The TLS_REJECT_UNAUTHORIZED=0 flag disables SSL validation — use only in dev environments
  • Check your company's AI policy before sending code to external APIs

My Experience

What surprised me most is how Claude handles the entire workflow autonomously. It doesn't just write code — it searches for the object, reads the source, understands the structure, reads class includes, creates the transport, locks the object, writes the code, runs a syntax check, activates, and unlocks. All from one natural language request.

 

Important Hint

It can make ABAP-specific mistakes (like forgetting RAP locking fields, or using reserved DDIC words). Always review AI-generated code before activating in production. But as a development accelerator and code reviewer, it's genuinely powerful.

References

  • GitHub: mcp-abap-adt — Mario Andreschak
  • GitHub: mcp-abap-abap-adt-api — Mario Andreschak
  • GitHub: sap-skills — 35 Claude Code plugins for SAP
  • Blog: SAP Docs MCP Server — Marian Zeis
  • Blog: Claude Code via MCP — SAP Community
  • Blog: Installing and Extending an ABAP MCP Server — SAP Community

I hope this helps other ABAP developers get started. If you run into issues or have questions, feel free to comment below. Happy coding!

entry.title: Re: SAP MBC - OAUTH 2.0 mTLS configuration on ERP side
entry.content:
In S/4HANA 2024, transaction OA2C_CONFIG is no longer the recommended approach for setting up OAuth 2.0 clients. SAP Multi‑Bank Connectivity now uses OAuth 2.0 with mTLS via SAP Cloud Identity Services (IAS) as the default and supported authentication method.
As a result, the configuration in newer releases will not look identical to older OA2C_CONFIG‑based documentation.

For current S/4HANA releases, the setup is based on:

  • SAP Cloud Identity Services (IAS) for OAuth client and certificate handling
  • Corporate Gateway / ERP Connectivity configuration on the ERP side
  • mTLS certificate management on the SAP Multi‑Bank Connectivity tenant

The legacy OA2C_CONFIG screenshots in some guides are therefore only conceptually valid but technically outdated for S/4HANA 2024.

Please refer to the following official SAP Help Portal documentation, which reflects the currently supported setup:

Configuring Authentication via OAuth 2.0 with mTLS (Default Method)

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_MULTI_BANK_CONNECTIVITY/a0fb81f60461411ba33bc586aecd3039/23fd4011e67c4981a37a06b325991612.html

Configuring Connectivity Between an ERP System and SAP Multi‑Bank Connectivity

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_MULTI_BANK_CONNECTIVITY/a0fb81f60461411ba33bc586aecd3039/ab5a61b1497848dd8b6b6669340cf882.html

Certificate Requirements for SAP Multi‑Bank Connectivity (OAuth mTLS, test vs. prod)

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_MULTI_BANK_CONNECTIVITY/3fa51d2862684bcfa3183bf499792673/00d1af2466d849a686f345186758c21a.html

Manage Certificate Assignments (mTLS certificates on MBC tenant – App F8801

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_MULTI_BANK_CONNECTIVITY/3fa51d2862684bcfa3183bf499792673/2c15e2d0a1a14a9fa012e4cbbe51b8f3.html

In summary, for S/4HANA 2024 systems, OAuth 2.0 with mTLS should be configured using IAS and the new OAuth framework, and differences to older OA2C_CONFIG‑based documentation are expected and intended.

entry.title: Re: update open delivery quantity even if a delivery bloc...
entry.content: By design, SAP does not update open credit values when a delivery block is set at schedule line level. To change this, either use header‑level blocks without confirmation, or implement a custom enhancement in credit update logic.

entry.title: SAP Business One Period-End Closing executes but no Journal Entry is created (no error)
entry.content:
Hi,

I am facing a critical issue with the Period-End Closing process in SAP Business One 9.2 and would appreciate your expert advice.

Current Situation:
I am trying to perform the Period-End Closing for the fiscal year 2025.

When I click the 'Execute' button in the Period-End Closing window, the status bar shows progress (it moves), but no Journal Entries (JE) are actually created.

There are no error messages or pop-ups. The process simply finishes with no results, and the account balances remain unchanged in the list.

I have already confirmed that:

All target accounts have balances in the grid.

The Posting Period for 2025 is set to 'Closing Period'.

G/L Account Determination (Retained Earnings and Period-End Closing accounts) is correctly configured.

Background (Previous Year History):
I suspect this might be related to what happened in the previous fiscal year (2024):

In 2024, I performed the Period-End Closing via the standard tool.

However, for certain reasons, I cancelled (reversed) the automatically generated JEs and then manually created JEs to close the accounts because the tool wouldn't let me re-run it at that time.

In the SQL Profiler, I also discovered that the system executes: EXEC sp_executesql N'DELETE T0 FROM [dbo].[CCPD] T0 WHERE T0.[PerAbs] = (@P1)', N'@P1 int', 109 right before the process stops.

I am wondering if this manual intervention in 2024 has corrupted the "closing flag" or the "Opening Balance" logic for 2025, preventing the tool from executing properly this year.

Questions:

1. Does manual closing in the previous year prevent the Period-End Closing tool from working in the subsequent year?

2. Is there a way to reset the "Closing" status or flags in the database so that the tool recognizes the 2025 balances as ready for closing?

Any help or troubleshooting steps would be greatly appreciated.

Best regards,

entry.title: Deployment Issue HANA HDI: schemaParameterChangeNotSupported
entry.content:
Hi All,
We have recently performed Node upgrade from 18 to 20 for few services in our landscape.

After deployment of service, below error is encountered:
"{"error":"schemaParameterChangeNotSupported","description":"Changing the 'schema' parameter for instance '' is not supported (is '<Subaccountname-spacename-servicename>', was '<some guid>')"}"

I have observed that initially for few services the mta.yaml did not have config schema name as follows and these services are having above mentioned error:

config:
schema: ${default-container-name}
 
These 2 lines were added sometime later after intial deployment of these services.
 
We have had many deployments of these services after this addition but only now, our deployment is resulting into the specified error.

For these services, we have HANA HDI schema name as some guid and for rest others we have name of HANA HDI schema as <SubaccountName-SpaceName-ServiceName>.

Please suggest how to fix/proceed.

@anshuman_s22 

Thanks.

entry.title: Integrating Slack with SAP Cloud Integration Using SAP Open Connectors
entry.content:
Introduction

In one of my recent SAP Cloud Integration scenarios, I needed to send real-time notifications to a Slack channel whenever an integration flow processed data successfully or encountered an issue. Instead of directly handling Slack APIs and OAuth authentication inside the iFlow, I decided to use SAP Open Connectors to simplify the integration.

In this blog, I’m sharing my personal experience integrating Slack with SAP Integration Suite (Cloud Integration) using SAP Open Connectors, including the steps I followed, what worked for me, and a few learnings along the way.

 

Step 1: Creating the Slack App and Channel

I started by creating a Slack app from the Slack API portal:  https://api.slack.com

For my use case, I only needed to post messages to a Slack channel, so I added the Bot Token Scope – chat:write. After configuring the scope, I installed the app into my Slack workspace.

Once the app was installed, Slack generated a Bot User OAuth Token. I copied this token, as it is required later for authentication.

I also ensured that the Slack app was added to the target Slack channel where messages would be posted.

 

  • Copy the generated Bot User OAuth Token

  1. Add the app to your Slack channel

SAP Cloud Integration Configuration

Step 2: Storing the Slack Token Securely in CPI

To follow security best practices, I did not hardcode the Slack token in my integration flow.

Instead, I navigated to:

Monitor → Manage Security Material → Create → Secure Parameter

Here, I stored the Slack Bot User OAuth Token securely. This made the iFlow more secure and easier to manage across environments.

Step 3: Creating a Slack Instance in SAP Open Connectors

Next, I moved to the SAP BTP Cockpit and navigated to:

Instances → SAP Open Connectors → Create Connector

From the list of available connectors, I selected Slack.

While configuring the instance, I entered the required OAuth scope (chat:write) and proceeded with the creation. During authorization, Slack prompted me to allow access, which I approved.

After successful authorization, the Slack connector instance was created.

 

Select the Slack connector.

 

Add OAuth Scope: chat:write

  • Click Create Instance
  • Authorize Slack → Click Allow

Step 4: Testing the Slack Connector Instance

Before using the connector in CPI, I tested it directly from Open Connectors → API Docs.

I used the following POST endpoint to test message posting:

/channels/{channelId}/messages

 

Use POST endpoint: /channels/{channelId}/messages

Step 5: Designing the Integration Flow (iFlow)

After confirming that the connector worked as expected, I designed the iFlow in SAP Cloud Integration.

  • I used a Content Modifier to define properties such as the Slack channel ID and message content.
  • I configured the Open Connector Receiver Adapter, selecting the Slack connector instance created earlier.
  • The secure parameter containing the Slack token was referenced indirectly via the connector, so no sensitive data appeared in the iFlow.

After deploying the iFlow, I triggered a test message and monitored the execution using the Message Monitor.

Use Content Modifier to create properties.

Configure the Open Connector Receiver Adapter.

Monitoring and Testing

I enabled tracing and observed the message flow execution. The Slack message was delivered successfully, confirming that the integration worked end-to-end.

Using SAP Open Connectors helped reduce complexity, especially around authentication and API handling, which would otherwise require custom logic.

Try Out

 

Conclusion

From my experience, integrating Slack with SAP Cloud Integration using SAP Open Connectors is a simple and effective approach for sending notifications and alerts from integration flows.

By using Open Connectors, I avoided direct API complexity and focused more on business logic. This setup worked well for real-time communication use cases and can be extended to monitoring, alerts, or business event notifications.

SAP Blog References

1️⃣Connecting SAP Integration Suite with Slack
https://community.sap.com/.../connecting-sap-integration-suite-with-slack

2️⃣Cloud Integration with Slack
https://community.sap.com/.../cloud-integration-with-slack

Reference Links

  1. SAP Help Portal – Cloud Integration
  2. Slack API – chat.postMessage

Regards,
Shalini Jayanthilal

entry.title: Re: How to get sales order not editable after invoice cre...
entry.content: In SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud, there is no standard configuration that automatically locks sales order fields (like payment terms or Incoterms) once an invoice has been created. By design, those fields remain editable in the sales order even after billing, because SAP assumes changes may be needed for subsequent deliveries or follow‑up documents. To meet the requirement, you need either a custom enhancement (preferred) or authorization control.

entry.title: Hands One - SAP Note: 3716130 Change in Root Certification Authority for SAP Default Certificate
entry.content:
*In collaboration with: @Muhammet_Tenbih 

Dear Community,

this blog provides practical guidance on how to navigate the upcoming change in the Root Certification Authority (CA)
for the SAP default client certificate in Cloud Integration and what actions you need to take before it affects your
integration scenarios.

Intention

If you are running Cloud Integration (part of SAP Integration Suite), you may have received an email notification
about an upcoming change to the SAP default key pair certificate.
Unlike a standard annual certificate renewal, this change also involves a switch of the certificate issuer from public
CA (DigiCert) to SAP's own CA ("SAP Cloud Root CA"). This distinction is important because it requires action on your
side that a normal renewal would not.

Background

Certification authorities (CAs) are phasing out the Client Authentication (ClientAuth) Extended Key Usage (EKU) from public SSL/TLS certificates due to a security-driven industry move to separate server and client roles. (Source: Client Authentication EKU Phased Out). To ensure continued support for mutual TLS (mTLS) client authentication, SAP is proactively switching to its own CA. 

  • Non-China data centers: SAP Cloud Root CA
  • China data centers: CDC Cloud Root CA

This means the trust chain of the SAP default client certificate will change. Any receiver system that validates the
certificate's chain of trust needs to be updated - otherwise your outbound integration scenarios will break with an
HTTP 401 response.

Timeline

The rollout starts April 15, 2026, but the effective date for your tenant depends on your current certificate's expiry
date.

ScenarioCert ExpiryOutcome
1After Apr 15, 2026 (e.g., July 1, 2026)New cert signed by SAP Cloud Root CA provisioned from Apr 15. Change takes effect on expiry or upon manual activation — whichever comes first.
2Before Apr 15, 2026 (e.g., Apr 07, 2026)New cert still signed by DigiCert.  SAP Cloud Root CA only
applies on the next renewal cycle (approx. one year later).

Solution & Recommendation

Outbound: Integration Suite (Cloud Integration) to Receiver 

Option 1: Recommendation

For outbound communication from CPI to receiver systems, we recommend using the SAP-managed default key pair (sap_cloudintegrationcertificate) for client certificate authentication. This certificate is automatically renewed by SAP and — following the ongoing CA transition to SAP Cloud Root CA - it will continue to support Client Authentication EKU, making it the most maintainable and roadmap-compliant option for mTLS-based outbound scenarios.

Option 2: Custom Client Certificate (if Receiver Does Not Trust SAP Cloud Root CA)

If the receiving system cannot be configured to trust SAP Cloud Root CA or CDC Cloud Root CA, the recommended fallback is to use a custom client certificate chain. Generate a Certificate Signing Request (CSR) — either via OpenSSL or directly from the SAP Integration Suite Keystore (recommended) — and have it signed by a Certificate Authority accepted by the receiving system. The resulting key pair is then uploaded to the CPI Keystore and referenced in the corresponding receiver adapter configuration.

Inbound: Sender to Integration Suite (Cloud Integration) 

Option 1: Recommendation

For inbound communication, we recommend using a client certificate signed by SAP Cloud Root CA (non-China) or CDC Cloud Root CA (China). This is the most maintainable and roadmap-compliant option, as these CAs are not subject to public CA EKU policy changes and will continue to support Client Authentication EKU beyond May 2026.

The certificate can be provisioned in two ways directly from the Process Integration Runtime (PIR) Service Key:

  • Standard: Generate the key pair directly from the PIR instance — the certificate is automatically signed by SAP Cloud Root CA and stored in the Service Key (screenshot 1)

  • External: Provide an externally generated CSR, have it signed by SAP Cloud Root CA via the Destination Service, and store the resulting certificate in the PIR Service Key (PIR Service Key / Role mapping). (Guide: SAP Integration Suite - Client Authentication EKU deprecation)

Option 2: Custom Client Certificate (External (approved/ not approved) Root CAs)

  • Approved CAs: If the sender system requires a certificate signed by a specific external CA, the certificate must be signed by a CA listed in SAP Note 2801396 (SAP Global Trust List). The corresponding key pair is maintained in the sender system's keystore and configured in the inbound authentication setup of the CPI service instance (PIR Service Key / Role mapping) (see Option 1, External).
  • Not Approved CAs: CAs outside the SAP Global Trust List are not accepted for inbound client certificate authentication under the standard domain. If a private or corporate CA outside this list is required, the SAP BTP Custom Domain Service must be used to configure a custom trust store for the CPI endpoint. (Guide: Using Self Signed certificates with SAP Custom Domain service for Integration Flow Authentication) 

Next Steps

Step by Step

  1. Identify affected iFlows
  2. Inventory your receiver systems
  3. Upload the new Root CA to receiver systems
  4. Test with the new certificate (in Dev/Test) 
  5. Activate in Production

Is There Anything Else to Consider?

  • Change management lead time is the most common risk here. Getting a Root CA uploaded to external or third-party
    systems can be a tough coordination. Start this process early — do not wait until the automatic activation deadline.
  • Professional Services are available if you need hands-on support with the impact assessment, trust store coordination, or testing. Reach out to your CSM or Account Executive as described in SAP KBA 2671448.

We'd love to hear your experiences and questions around this change. Are there specific receiver systems or scenarios
where you're unsure about the impact? Feel free to reach out via SAP Integration Services.

---

#Modernize #Integrate #Innovate – SAP Integration Modernization TEAM

entry.title: Re: How to set up the initial the Accrued Revenue and Def...
entry.content:
Hello @Feng_Chen 

Thank you for your question.

For the enterprise project scenario with scope item 6GD and revenue recognition key EPMCC, Event-Based Revenue Recognition-Projects does not provide a separate migration function that will enable your client to manually create opening accrued revenue and deferred revenue balances for a migrated project. That app shows recognised values that are associated with project-based transactions, and the balances are calculated and updated by the EBRR process, especially through Run Revenue Recognition-Projects.

Here are references for more guiding details on Revenue Recognition methods.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/89d896ca9cd64318b1667df5ec00e4b2/7fd9ad1108b94403a2871cf348d4b891.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/89d896ca9cd64318b1667df5ec00e4b2/23cee4ab4c5443c6b68e274e69dcf19f.html?locale=en-US

The standard supported process is:

  1. For you to migrate the enterprise project and relevant source data.

See the following references for migration support. https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/d5699934e7004d048c4801b552f3b013/444fa56a65a84453bbf5e306d754a3b3.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/d5699934e7004d048c4801b552f3b013/20607b6de19a4d34a41b0abec6186b01.html?locale=en-US

  1. Use Event-Based Revenue Recognition-Projects app to validate the EBRR configuration on the migrated project. In the app, search for the migrated project or billing WBS, confirm that the revenue recognition key is EPMCC. Consult KBA 3656776 for more details.
  2. Use Run Revenue Recognition-Projects to execute the process for the relevant period and project section. Then review the result in Event-Based Revenue Recognition-Projects app.

Here are references for more guidance

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/89d896ca9cd64318b1667df5ec00e4b2/c25a797f5ccb4690a4700e324fbe8662.html?locale=en-US

Therefore, if your customer expects the app itself to create opening accrued revenue and deferred revenue without migrated project-based source transaction, the Public Cloud does not support that process. The standard design remains that EBRR derives those balances from the migrated operational project data and the revenue recognition run, not from a separate opening balance load inside the app.

Regarding your request for a blog, maybe you can have a look at the following blog which provides details on project migration.

Customer Projects Migration - Part 1: Migration of Fixed Price Projects and Time & Expense Projects

I hope this provides some details you can use for your client.

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: Question on Automatic Clearing Rules – Do All Selecte...
entry.content:
You are correct in your understanding. In OB74 Automatic Clearing configuration, SAP requires that all criteria defined in a single clearing rule must match simultaneously for clearing to occur. If you specify five fields, then all five must align between the debit and credit items before the system will clear them.

One option is you can create separate entries in OB74, each with its own combination of fields.

Example:

  • Rule 1: Amount + Reference
  • Rule 2: Amount + Billing Document
  • Rule 3: Amount + Assignment

SAP will then evaluate each rule independently during clearing.

On the other hand, if you need true “OR” logic within a single rule, that requires custom development (BTE/exit).

entry.title: SAP SuccessFactors Latest People Profile: Workaround for Error 400 Caused by Child Object References
entry.content:
SAP SuccessFactors Latest People Profile: Workaround for Error 400 Caused by Child Object References

Introduction:

With the adoption of the Latest People Profile in SAP SuccessFactors, you may notice that configurations that previously worked in legacy screens no longer behave the same or may not work at all.

One such scenario arises when working with the Metadata Framework (MDF), specifically with composite (parent-child) objects. A common expectation is the ability to reuse child objects as value sources or references in different MDF objects.

However, as highlighted in SAP KBA 3675213 (Issue #3), this approach is not supported in the Latest People Profile:

"Referring (value source) a child object in a Parent Generic Object field is not supported."

Reference: https://me.sap.com/notes/0003675213

This blog explores this limitation in detail, explains the underlying design principles behind it, and provides a suggested workaround. This workaround may address some scenarios, depending on why you chose to reference a child object in a parent generic object that is not the same parent.

To make this clear, consider the images below:

Before enabling Latest People Profile, everything works as expected.

Legacy Employee Profile showing the "Demo Selection" block working correctly before the update.

The legacy "Demo Selection" edit dialog with a functional dropdown menu for child objects.

After enabling Latest People Profile, you may encounter an error like:

“We could not process your request as the entity cust_DemoSelection has unsupported fields. Update or remove these fields from the object definition. See details: fieldName: cust_DemoDocument, fieldDataType: GO.”

The image below illustrates this behavior, showing how a previously valid configuration now triggers this error.

Latest People Profile displaying "Error Code: 400" due to unsupported child object fields.


Configuration That Triggers the Limitation

This issue occurs with a configuration that was previously valid but is no longer supported in the Latest People Profile. The key factor is that a child object is being referenced in a field of another object, and the child is configured as “From Parent”.

SAP has confirmed in an enhancement request that this behavior will not be developed, stating explicitly:

“Any object with effective dated as FROM_PARENT must not be used outside its parent object.”

Reference: https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/365909

Parent-Child Object Structure

Object Name

Object Type

Relationship / Association

Notes

cust_DemoCategory

Generic Object

Parent

Holds categories; child documents reside within this parent

cust_DemoDocument

Generic Object

Composite Child of cust_DemoCategory

Each document is linked to its parent category

Object definition showing cust_DemoDocument configured as a "From Parent" composite child.

This image shows that cust_DemoDocument is a composite child, inheriting its context from cust_DemoCategory. It cannot exist independently, which is central to why referencing it elsewhere now triggers an error.

Field Referencing the Child Object

Object Name

Field Name

Field Type / Reference

Notes

cust_DemoSelection

cust_DemoDocument

Generic Object (GO) Reference

Points to cust_DemoDocument (child of cust_DemoCategory)

 

Field details in cust_DemoSelection showing the unsupported reference to a child object.

This image highlights that cust_DemoDocument is a composite child “From Parent”, meaning it exists only within the context of cust_DemoCategory. This is a key reason why referencing it in another object now triggers an error.

Workaround: Using an Independent Object

It’s important to note that this workaround may not work for every client, as its suitability depends on why you initially referenced the child object.

Moreover, since simply changing the association of the existing “From Parent” object would result in losing historical data, the recommended approach is to create a new independent object

To maintain relationships or structure, the new independent object can be associated using Valid When, similar to how a Department is associated with a Division. This preserves the connection between objects while allowing flexible referencing with no error on the Latest People Profile.

Definition of the new cust_DemoDocumentIndep object set to "Basic" effective dating.


Add the following association in the cust_DemoDocumentIndep object definition:

Association to DemoCategory Object (Higher Level)

Name

Multiplicity

Destination Object

Type

cust_toDemoCategory

One To One

Demo Category

Valid When

 

Manage Data screen showing the new independent object linked via a "Valid When" association.

Data for the new independent object (e.g., cust_DemoDocumentIndep) can be maintained either directly in Manage Data for small volumes or using Import and Export Data for mass uploads, while keeping it linked to the Demo Category.

Updating References in DemoSelection

Once the new independent object (e.g., cust_DemoDocumentIndep) is created, it needs to be referenced in the DemoSelection object instead of the original “From Parent” child object.

Configuration update changing the "Valid Values Source" to the new independent object.

Important: Before updating the reference, export the data from the DemoSelection object. Changing the reference will clear existing field values, so you will need to re-import or update these records to restore the data after the new reference is applied.

Verification in Employee Profile

After updating the reference in the cust_DemoSelection object to point to the new independent object (cust_DemoDocument_Indep), checking the Employee Profile shows that the previous error no longer appears.

Historical data in the original object is maintained; however, the reference field in DemoSelection will be empty and needs to be updated using a mass update or re-import to restore the correct values.

Successful loading of the "Demo Selection" block in the Latest People Profile after the fix.

User profile change history showing the reference field is empty and requires a data re-import.

Final verification of the functional dropdown menu in the Latest People Profile interface.

This change must be completed before the Production Go-Live (May 15–16), as the Latest People Profile will be universally enforced with no option to disable it and does not support referencing “From Parent” child objects outside their parent context.

SAP Proactive Validation Message

SAP has introduced a system-level check to prevent this configuration error from being saved in the future. When attempting to reference a "From Parent" child object, the Configure Object Definitions tool now triggers a message alerting the consultant to the limitation before the configuration is finalized.

SAP SuccessFactors validation message in "Configure Object Definitions" warning against referencing "From Parent" child objects.

Notes & Key Considerations

  • Ensure a clear cutover date is defined, and temporarily remove employee permissions during the transition to avoid unintended changes.
  • Confirm that there are no pending workflows, as in-progress records will not appear in report/export or import processes.
  • Maintain a data backup or reference extract of the object before making any changes to safeguard against accidental deletion or incorrect updates.
  • Evaluate whether a Picklist (Parent-Child structure) could meet the requirement, as it may provide a simpler and more supported alternative in some scenarios.

entry.title: HRFORM - Sending Payslips to Individual Employees from SAP to Outlook or Mails - for all countries
entry.content:
Introduction


Are you an ABAP developer working in the HCM module, HR ABAP? Explore this blog to learn how to create a custom program for sending employee pay slips by mail.


Requirement


Even though Payslips are accessible in EC, clients will often ask us to send them by mail as attachments.

Challenges

1. There are no standard reports available for all the countries to send payslips by mail, like the one for India. 
2. Also, developing a report from scratch is very complex for HR Forms.



Steps to create a custom report



1. We are not going to create a report from Scratch; SAP already developed a report for India HINU_HRFORMS_MAIL, but for other countries, standard reports are not available.

Changes in the report: Change the default constant and parameter values (your custom HR Form name, country grouping, payroll area for your country, etc).

Code for your reference

 

CONSTANTS : gc_applogname    TYPE balobj_d VALUE 'ZHRPAYSLIP',
            gc_applogsubname TYPE balsubobj VALUE 'ZHRFORM_MAIL'.
PARAMETERS: p_molga          LIKE thrforms_01-molga VALUE CHECK DEFAULT '24',
            p_fclass         TYPE hrf_class VALUE CHECK DEFAULT 'PAYSLIP', "thrforms_01-formclass
            p_fname          LIKE thrforms_01-infonet OBLIGATORY DEFAULT 'ZHR_PAYSLIP'.

 


2. This report will work after implementing the BADI HRFORM_HRF02; otherwise, we can only display the payslip.

3. Write your own logic to send mail in the method AFTER_PDF_PRINT. Also sample implementation class is available for our reference.

Report Analysis

1. This is a SUBMIT program of the HRFORM report.

2. It needs a BADI implementation of HRFORM_HRF024.

3. Functionality is the same as HRFORM display tcode, e.g., PC00_M24_HRF, etc.

4. Designed with SLG1 log. (log gets updated in SLG1 after running the report).


Results & thoughts


With the help of the HINU_HRFORMS_MAIL report, we can develop our custom report to send a payslip as an attachment to all employees. And it would be better if SAP had given a standard report for other countries, too or else any SAP Support Note for reference, mentioning this report.

entry.title: Re: Sub contracting
entry.content:
Practical mapping options

ERP Subcontracting Process:
Use subcontracting POs for external operations, with material issue and receipt flows.

Production Orders:
For internal operations, use production orders with goods issue and receipt postings.

Batch Determination:
Configure batch determination so that the correct batch flows through each operation.

Reporting:
Use batch-wise stock reports (e.g., MB52 in SAP or equivalent) to show stock at each stage.

The key decision is whether you want one continuous batch number across all four operations (strong traceability) or different batch numbers per stage (clearer stock separation). If your client’s priority is visibility at each stage, separate material codes with batch tracking is usually cleaner.
